summary: This notebook demonstrates how to run the GPT-OSS-20b large language model on Kaggle's GPU runtime by resolving environment dependency conflicts. It walks through uninstalling pre-installed packages and installing specific compatible versions of PyTorch, Transformers, and Triton before downloading the model via Kaggle Hub. The author then initializes a Hugging Face text-generation pipeline and executes a sample inference call to verify functionality. Readers learn how to navigate package versioning challenges on Kaggle to successfully deploy large open-weight models for inference.
competition_flow: Installs compatible dependencies, downloads the GPT-OSS-20b model via Kaggle Hub, runs a text-generation inference pipeline, and prints the generated response.
insights: Resolving package version conflicts is often necessary when running large models on Kaggle's pre-configured environments.
Kaggle Hub provides a straightforward way to download and cache large model weights directly in notebook cells.
Hugging Face's pipeline API abstracts away complex inference boilerplate, enabling quick deployment of open-weight LLMs.
